State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest

(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

November 15, 1979

Ms. Nancy Boyer, Chairman
Pacific Residents For Oceanside
524 S. Myers St.
Oceanside, CA 92054

Dear Ms. Boyer:

     Your letter to the Attorney General's office regarding
former City Councilman Douglas M. Avis has been referred to
the Fair Political Practices Commission for review and con-
sideration.  Set forth below are the facts as I understand
them.

     While he was member of the City Council, Mr. Avis was
also a director of the Oceanside Redevelopment Agency.  He
resigned his seats on the council and redevelopment agency
and has since been retained by the Condor Corp., Century
Southwest Corporation and Warren Lusarde Construction Company
who are proposing a major project in the redevelopment area
which must be approved by the redevelopment agency.

     The question that you have asked is whether it is per-
missible for Mr. Avis to represent the companies before the
redevelopment agency, and if so, whether there is a period
of time after his resignation during which he must refrain
from doing so.  Government Code Section 87100 provides that:

     No public official at any level of state or
local government shall make, participate in making
or in any way attempt to use his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.

                                        (Emphasis added.)

     "Public official" is defined by Government Code Section
82048 to mean:

     "Public official" means every member, officer,
employee or consultant of a state or local govern-
ment agency.

MS. Nancy Boyer, Chairman
November 15, 1979
Page Two


     Mr. Avis has resigned his government position, and is
therefore no longer a public official within the meaning of
the Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000-
91014. Its disqualification provisions therefore no longer
restrain his actions. In addition, there exists no require-
ment in this Act that Mr. Avis temporarily refrain from
appearing before the redevelopment agency because of his
prior service as a director of the agency. I do not know if
there are any local ordinances which would address these
questions.

     If you have any questions regarding the Political Reform
Act, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dwight E. Dickerson
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DED:plh

Pacific Residents for Oceanside
Nancy Boyer, Chairman
524 S. Myers St.
Oceanside, CA 92054
(714) 722-5185


Office of the Attorney General
555 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, CA 95814
c/o Government Law Section

Dear Sir:

     We are a citizens group which is currently active in a
redevelopment area in the city of Oceanside.

     A recent resignation by an Oceanside city councilman has
raised several questions of propriety in our minds.  Because of our
interest in redevelopment, we are extremely concerned.

     We are enclosing two(2) newspaper articles which give an
account of this councilman's resignation and future association.
We are also enclosing the unofficial transcript of a portion of
the Oceanside City Council meeting held on Aug. 6, 1979 and a
statement of economic interests filed Mar. 20, 1979 pages 1,2,4,6,9.

     According to the newspaper articles, this councilman, who was
also a redevelopment agency member has resigned and accepted a
position with Condor Corp. which has a contract pending before the
redevelopment agency for the right to design, and perhaps, to receive
a portion of the redevelopment area for future development.

     Our questions are these: Does a redevelopment agency member
have the right to join a development firm dealing with the project
area?  How long after getting off the redevelopment agency must a
former member refrain from business dealings within the project area?

     Because many of us in Pacific Residents for Oceanside are
property owners within the project area, the answers to these
questions will have a direct effect on our future.  We would
appreciate your looking into this matter for us.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Nancy Boyer, Chairman
                                        Pacific Residents for Oceanside

State of California

Memorandum

To : Ted, Bob S.                                    Date : October 11, 1979


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dwight Dickerson

Subject: Nancy Boyer letter to Attorney General's office (Referral
         from Attorney General's Public Inquiry Unit)

Ms. Boyer raised two questions in her letter to the Attorney
General's office. First, does a redevelopment agency member have
the right to join a development firm that is doing business in the
agency's project area?

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit the agency member
from joining the redevelopment firm. However, if the agency member
joins the firm or is promised employment while serving in office,
Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 are applicable.

Ms. Boyer's second question asks whether there is a period of
time in which an exmember of a redevelopment agency must refrain
from doing business with the agency or in the project area established
by the agency for development. The Political Reform Act contains
no restrictions on the activities of officials who leave office.
Therefore, the exofficial may do business with us or her former
agency unless prohibited by another provision of state law.

Although there are no conflict of interest violations raised
by Ms. Boyer's inquiry, Mr. Avis may have committed several reporting
violations. According to facts presented by Ms. Boyer and newspaper
articles, realtor Douglas M. Avis is a former member of the Oceanside
City Council and redevelopment agency. In August, Mr. Avis resigned
his seat to represent a group of developers that are proposing
projects in the Oceanside redevelopment area. Newspaper articles
indicate he had previously represented one of the developers (Condor
Corporation) in a condominium conversion. (It is unclear from the
facts presented when and where the condo-conversion took place.)
Mr. Avis' Statement of Economic Interests does not reflect that he
provided services to Condor.

There were other questions raised by Mr. Avis' annual statement.
Mr. Avis' reported real property holdings that are valued in the
hundreds of thousands of dollars; however, his income schedule
shows very little income received during the same period. One
piece of property was disposed of by Mr. Avis but the buyer of the
property was not disclosed. The property's fair market value
exceeded $100,000 according to his disclosure statement. Mr. Avis
did not disclose any customers of his real estate business which
is odd because he's a sole proprietor.

DED:plh

State of California                                555 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 550
                                                             SACRAMENTO 95814
Department of Justice                                        (916) 322-3360
George Deukmejian
PRONOUNCED DUKE-MAY-GIN

Attorney General


Fair Political Practices Commission                September 11, 1979
Conflict of Interest Division
1100 "K" Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: LETTER RECEIVED BY PUBLIC INQUIRY UNIT

          The enclosed letter concerns a matter which does not come
under the jurisdiction of the Attorney General's Office. We are,
therefore, referring it to you for reply.

          The correspondent has been informed of our action.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN
                                                Attorney General

                                                [signature]
                                                TONY CIMARUSTT
                                                Chief, Information Services

FORM 721

Statement of Economic Interests

(Government Code Sections 87200 et seq.)

This form is for officeholders filing annual
statements, newly elected state office holders
assuming office in December or January, and
officials who have left office.

See "Instruction Manual for Statement of Economic Interest—Form 721."

Please type or print in ink.

1. Name: DOUGLAS M. DAVIS

2. Address: 911 MISTRAL AVE.

3. Office held: CITY COUNCILMAN CITY OF OCEANSIDE
(e.g., county supervisor, city council member, etc.)

State of

[X] This is my annual statement. (State officers, including judges, file between January 1 and
March 1; city and county officials file between January 1 and April 1.)

[ ] I left the above-named office as of__________________.
(File within 30 days of leaving office.)                  (Date)

5. Period covered by the statement:

1 — [ ] — [illegible] to 12 — 31 — 75
Mo.        Day        Year        Mo.        Day        Year

VERIFICATION

I declare under penalty of perjury that I have used reasonable diligence in preparing this statement
and to the best of my knowledge is true and proper.

Executed on 3/20 , 19[illegible], at OCEANSIDE , California.

_________________________
(Signature)

NOTE to officers receiving statements: Except for state officers and chief administrative officers, which should be retained
by you, make and retain copies of all statements filed with you and forward the originals within five days of the filing deadline (or if filed late,
within five days of receipt to: The Fair Political Practices Commission, Conflict of Interest Division, P.O. Box 807, Sacramento, California
95814.

sections.question: Does a redevelopment agency member have the right to join a development firm dealing with the project area? How long after getting off the redevelopment agency must a former member refrain from business dealings within the project area?
sections.conclusion: Mr. Avis has resigned his government position, and is therefore no longer a public official within the meaning of the Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Its disqualification provisions therefore no longer restrain his actions. In addition, there exists no requirement in this Act that Mr. Avis temporarily refrain from appearing before the redevelopment agency because of his prior service as a director of the agency.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a former city councilman and redevelopment agency director may represent development companies with projects pending before the redevelopment agency, and whether there is a required cooling-off period after resignation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit former officials from representing private interests before their former agency, and contains no cooling-off period requirement. However, potential reporting violations regarding undisclosed financial interests may exist.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple components: original citizen inquiry, Attorney General referral, FPPC memorandum analysis, and supplemental materials (transcript excerpt, Form 721). Main advice letter response is from Dwight E. Dickerson dated November 15, 1979. Some ambiguity in document structure due to lack of clear section headers.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a redevelopment agency member have the right to join a development firm dealing with the project area? How long after getting off the redevelopment agency must a former member refrain from business dealings within the project area?

CONCLUSION: Mr. Avis has resigned his government position, and is therefore no longer a public official within the meaning of the Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Its disqualification provisions therefore no longer restrain his actions. In addition, there exists no requirement in this Act that Mr. Avis temporarily refrain from appearing before the redevelopment agency because of his prior service as a director of the agency.

Avis has been referred to the Fair Political Practices Commission for review and con- sideration. Set forth below are the facts as I understand them. While he was member of the City Council, Mr. Avis was also a director of the Oceanside Redevelopment Agency. He resigned his seats on the council and redevelopment agency and has since been retained by the Condor Corp., Century Southwest Corporation and Warren Lusarde Construction Company who are proposing a major project in the redevelopment area which must be approved by the redevelopment agency. The question that you have asked is whether it is per- missible for Mr. Avis to represent the companies before the redevelopment agency, and if so, whether there is a period of time after his resignation during which he must refrain from doing so. Government Code Section 87100 provides that: No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. (Emphasis added.) "Public official" is defined by Government Code Section 82048 to mean: "Public official" means every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local govern- ment agency. embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a citizen inquiry about whether former Oceanside City Councilman Douglas M. Avis may represent developers before the redevelopment agency after his resignation. The Commission concludes that the Political Reform Act imposes no prohibition or waiting period on such activity, though it identifies potential reporting violations in Avis's Statement of Economic Interests.
